channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Red Cross warns Ebola outbreak in DRC not yet peaked, could last a year
item.description: The Red Cross says it is 'very difficult' to know the extent at which the epidemic is spreading.

item.title: India blocks Telegram until Monday due to student exam fraud concerns
item.description: A viral youth satirical protest movement, the Cockroach Janta Party, has emerged following exam cancellations last month

item.title: Families return to shattered towns as fragile ceasefire holds in Lebanon
item.description: Southern Lebanon’s displaced face dire conditions as they return to salvaged belongings amid ceasefire uncertainties.

item.title: Gaza children running out of time to treat blast-induced hearing loss
item.description: Gaza children increasingly developing hearing impairments due to repeated exposure to blasts.

item.title: Suffragettes to Palestine Action: A history of direct-action protest in UK
item.description: Analysts say Britain is responding to civil disobedience with unprecedented severity.

item.title: Messi, Argentina begin World Cup title defence against Algeria: All to know
item.description: Superstar Lionel Messi is back for a sixth World Cup and leads Argentina into their group match debut against Algeria.

item.title: Trump tells Netanyahu to be ‘more responsible with respect to Lebanon’
item.description: US president also made unusually critical comments about Israel's military offensive in Lebanon.

item.title: Will a US-Iran deal unlock $300bn in investment fund for Tehran?
item.description: The Trump administration has been adamant the investment fund is not a payout for Iranian enriched uranium.

item.title: Trump says ‘hell will rain down’ if Iran gets nuclear weapons
item.description: Trump says ‘hell will rain down’ if Iran gets nuclear weapons

item.title: Qatar’s Emir hails Iran deal and touts US investments in Trump meeting
item.description: Qatar’s Emir hails Iran deal and touts US investments in Trump meeting.

item.title: Australia warns El Nino weather pattern set to be strongest in decades
item.description: Australia’s Bureau of Meteorology says forecasts have pointed towards a 'very strong El Nino event'.

item.title: China offers staunch support to Myanmar president during his state visit
item.description: The two countries sign 18 cooperation deals, including on free trade and assistance in the event of natural disasters.

item.title: World Cup: Can Senegal stun France again? Predictions, schedule on Day 6
item.description: France take on Senegal in a repeat of their famous 2002 opener while Messi's Argentina face Algeria in Kansas City.

item.title: Iran says Israeli occupation in Lebanon would breach US deal
item.description: Iran says any continued Israeli occupation in Lebanon would be a breach of the Memorandum of Understanding with the US.

item.title: G7 leaders meet in France with Iran and Ukraine high on agenda
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said Tuesday’s lunch summit would focus on reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iran fans react to team’s first World Cup match amid political schisms
item.description: Iran fans react to team’s first World Cup match amid political schisms

item.title: What to know about this week’s US primary elections
item.description: Primaries are set to take place in Alabama, California, Oklahoma, Georgia, and the capital, Washington, DC, on Tuesday.

item.title: Football fans celebrate the World Cup across continents and cultures
item.description: Fans in jerseys and painted faces gather to watch World Cup matches, celebrating unity and rivalry alike.

item.title: Former CAR president on trial in absentia over crimes against humanity
item.description: The alleged crimes committed by Bozize's security forces include murder, enforced disappearance, torture and rape.

item.title: Eight people killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash: What we know
item.description: The aircraft, one of the US Air Force's longest-used bombers, goes down during a routine test mission.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: World sanctions settlers, Israel funds settlements
item.description: Amnesty calls West Bank ethnic cleansing 'state-sponsored' and international allies bar Finance Minister Smotrich.

item.title: Israel uses ‘battlefield evidence’ to prosecute Palestinians abroad
item.description: Palestinian activists are being put on trial in Europe for supporting Hamas on the basis of Israeli evidence.

item.title: ‘My voice is being heard’: A month of India’s Cockroach Janta Party
item.description: Satirical party launches nationwide Gen Z protest to demand federal education minister's removal over exam paper leaks.

item.title: Iran war day 109: Tehran, Washington, sign MoU electronically
item.description: Iran’s National Security Council said on Monday that the deal ends fighting on all fronts, including in Lebanon.

item.title: Japan’s central bank raises interest rates to highest level since 1995
item.description: Bank of Japan hikes benchmark rate to 1 percent, continuing shift away from decades of ultra-low borrowing costs.
